figures.caption: Figure 1: NARCBENCH-STEGO: four LLM agents play blackjack. The Counter tracks the card count and encodes it in natural table talk; the Big Player decodes the signal and bets accordingly. Honest agents are unaware of the scheme. Each shoe uses a unique encoding negotiated in a private channel.
Table 1: AUROC (↑) across NARCBENCH tiers. Deception probe trained on CORE, evaluated zero-shot on TRANSFER and STEGO. Results are means across layers 26–30. Bold = best per row.
Figure 2: AUROC for five probing techniques across NARCBENCH tiers. Deception probe trained on Core, evaluated zero-shot on Transfer and Stego. Error bars show standard deviation across layers 26–30.

Figure 3: Token-level analysis of NARCBENCH-STEGO. (a) Mean deception-direction projection for the big player (+0.29) vs. honest agents (−1.41) reading the same counter message, across 39 matched examples. (b) Schematic per-token heatmap: the big player’s activations are elevated at signal words while the honest agent’s remain flat.
